# Philosophy & Social Theory Review — Author Guidelines (Synthetic Fixture)

**Journal:** Philosophy & Social Theory Review
**Publisher:** Routledge / Taylor & Francis
**ISSN:** 1234-5678 (print), 2345-6789 (online)
**URL:** https://www.example-journal.com/pstr

## Aims and Scope

Philosophy & Social Theory Review is an international peer-reviewed journal
publishing original research in philosophy, social theory, critical theory,
and institutional analysis. The journal welcomes theoretical essays,
conceptual articles, and interdisciplinary work at the intersection of
philosophy, education, technology, and social institutions.

Topics of particular interest include: epistemology, philosophy of education,
philosophy of technology, institutional theory, democratic theory,
and the philosophy of social sciences.

## Article Types

1. Research articles (6,000-10,000 words)
2. Theoretical essays (5,000-8,000 words)
3. Book reviews (2,000-4,000 words)
4. Critical commentaries (3,000-5,000 words)

## Submission Requirements

- **Language:** English only
- **Abstract:** 150-250 words
- **Keywords:** 4-6
- **Word count:** 5,000-10,000 words for full articles
- **Citation style:** APA 7th edition
- **File format:** .docx or .pdf

## Language Policy

The journal accepts manuscripts in English only. Abstracts must be in English.

## Peer Review

- **Model:** Double-blind peer review
- **Timeline:** Initial decision within 8 weeks
- **Anonymization:** Remove all author-identifying information

## AI Policy

Authors must disclose use of AI writing tools in a dedicated statement.

## Data Availability

A data availability statement is required for empirical research.

## Ethics

Authors must declare conflicts of interest. Research involving human
subjects requires ethics approval documentation.
